name: Special Golden Loach
description: A legendary freshwater amphibian that can spit out pearls. They are found mostly where the water is clear and pure. Compared with ordinary Golden Loaches, this variety possesses a noticeably richer golden hue. This may be related to the concentration of kuuvahki in the waters where it resides.\nThey say that Golden Loaches are the favored children of the river itself. Perhaps it is because they are cradled by the river's gentle embrace that they wear such perpetual, guileless smiles.\nThey ingest indigestible elements, gravel, and mineral ores, sequestering them within their bodies to be forged into sparkling, shining pearls — a glittering defense they expel to dazzle and distract any predator.
